verses.latin_vulgate: Jacobus, Dei et Domini nostri Jesu Christi servus, duodecim tribubus, quæ sunt in dispersione, salutem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰάκωβος θεοῦ καὶ κυρίου ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ δοῦλος ταῖς δώδεκα φυλαῖς ταῖς ἐν τῇ διασπορᾷ χαίρειν
verses.king_james: James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes which are scattered abroad, greeting.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omne gaudium existimate fratres mei, cum in tentationes varias incideritis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶσαν χαρὰν ἡγήσασθε ἀδελφοί μου ὅταν πειρασμοῖς περιπέσητε ποικίλοις
verses.king_james: My brethren, count it all joy when ye fall into divers temptations;

verses.latin_vulgate: scientes quod probatio fidei vestræ patientiam operatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γινώσκοντες ὅτι τὸ δοκίμιον ὑμῶν τῆς πίστεως κατεργάζεται ὑπομονήν
verses.king_james: Knowing this, that the trying of your faith worketh patience.

verses.latin_vulgate: Patientia autem opus perfectum habet: ut sitis perfecti et integri in nullo deficientes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ δὲ ὑπομονὴ ἔργον τέλειον ἐχέτω ἵνα ἦτε τέλειοι καὶ ὁλόκληροι ἐν μηδενὶ λειπόμενοι
verses.king_james: But let patience have her perfect work, that ye may be perfect and entire, wanting nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis autem vestrum indiget sapientia, postulet a Deo, qui dat omnibus affluenter, et non improperat: et dabitur ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δέ τις ὑμῶν λείπεται σοφίας αἰτείτω παρὰ τοῦ διδόντος θεοῦ πᾶσιν ἁπλῶς καὶ μὴ ὀνειδίζοντος καὶ δοθήσεται αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally, and upbraideth not; and it shall be given him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Postulet autem in fide nihil hæsitans: qui enim hæsitat, similis est fluctui maris, qui a vento movetur et circumfertur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αἰτείτω δὲ ἐν πίστει μηδὲν διακρινόμενος ὁ γὰρ διακρινόμενος ἔοικεν κλύδωνι θαλάσσης ἀνεμιζομένῳ καὶ ῥιπιζομένῳ
verses.king_james: But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering. For he that wavereth is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed.

verses.latin_vulgate: non ergo æstimet homo ille quod accipiat aliquid a Domino.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ γὰρ οἰέσθω ὁ ἄνθρωπος ἐκεῖνος ὅτι λήψεταί τι παρὰ τοῦ κυρίου
verses.king_james: For let not that man think that he shall receive any thing of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir duplex animo inconstans est in omnibus viis suis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνὴρ δίψυχος ἀκατάστατος ἐν πάσαις ταῖς ὁδοῖς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: A double minded man is unstable in all his ways.

verses.latin_vulgate: Glorietur autem frater humilis in exaltatione sua:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καυχάσθω δὲ ὁ ἀδελφὸς ὁ ταπεινὸς ἐν τῷ ὕψει αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Let the brother of low degree rejoice in that he is exalted:

verses.latin_vulgate: dives autem in humilitate sua, quoniam sicut flos fœni transibit;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ πλούσιος ἐν τῇ ταπεινώσει αὐτοῦ ὅτι ὡς ἄνθος χόρτου παρελεύσεται
verses.king_james: But the rich, in that he is made low: because as the flower of the grass he shall pass away.

verses.latin_vulgate: exortus est enim sol cum ardore, et arefecit fœnum, et flos ejus decidit, et decor vultus ejus deperiit: ita et dives in itineribus suis marcescet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνέτειλεν γὰρ ὁ ἥλιος σὺν τῷ καύσωνι καὶ ἐξήρανεν τὸν χόρτον καὶ τὸ ἄνθος αὐτοῦ ἐξέπεσεν καὶ ἡ εὐπρέπεια τοῦ προσώπου αὐτοῦ ἀπώλετο οὕτως καὶ ὁ πλούσιος ἐν ταῖς πορείαις αὐτοῦ μαρανθήσεται
verses.king_james: For the sun is no sooner risen with a burning heat, but it withereth the grass, and the flower thereof falleth, and the grace of the fashion of it perisheth: so also shall the rich man fade away in his ways.

verses.latin_vulgate: Beatus vir qui suffert tentationem: quoniam cum probatus fuerit, accipiet coronam vitæ, quam repromisit Deus diligentibus se.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μακάριος ἀνὴρ ὃς ὑπομένει πειρασμόν ὅτι δόκιμος γενόμενος λήψεταί τὸν στέφανον τῆς ζωῆς ὃν ἐπηγγείλατο ὁ κύριος τοῖς ἀγαπῶσιν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Blessed is the man that endureth temptation: for when he is tried, he shall receive the crown of life, which the Lord hath promised to them that love him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo cum tentatur, dicat quoniam a Deo tentatur: Deus enim intentator malorum est: ipse autem neminem tentat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μηδεὶς πειραζόμενος λεγέτω ὅτι ἀπὸ τοῦ θεοῦ πειράζομαι ὁ γὰρ θεὸς ἀπείραστός ἐστιν κακῶν πειράζει δὲ αὐτὸς οὐδένα
verses.king_james: Let no man say when he is tempted, I am tempted of God: for God cannot be tempted with evil, neither tempteth he any man:

verses.latin_vulgate: Unusquisque vero tentatur a concupiscentia sua abstractus, et illectus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕκαστος δὲ πειράζεται ὑπὸ τῆς ἰδίας ἐπιθυμίας ἐξελκόμενος καὶ δελεαζόμενος
verses.king_james: But every man is tempted, when he is drawn away of his own lust, and enticed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deinde concupiscentia cum conceperit, parit peccatum: peccatum vero cum consummatum fuerit, generat mortem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶτα ἡ ἐπιθυμία συλλαβοῦσα τίκτει ἁμαρτίαν ἡ δὲ ἁμαρτία ἀποτελεσθεῖσα ἀποκύει θάνατον
verses.king_james: Then when lust hath conceived, it bringeth forth sin: and sin, when it is finished, bringeth forth death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite itaque errare, fratres mei dilectissimi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ πλανᾶσθε ἀδελφοί μου ἀγαπητοί
verses.king_james: Do not err, my beloved brethren.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omne datum optimum, et omne donum perfectum desursum est, descendens a Patre luminum, apud quem non est transmutatio, nec vicissitudinis obumbratio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶσα δόσις ἀγαθὴ καὶ πᾶν δώρημα τέλειον ἄνωθέν ἐστιν καταβαῖνον ἀπὸ τοῦ πατρὸς τῶν φώτων παρ᾽ ᾧ οὐκ ἔνι παραλλαγὴ ἢ τροπῆς ἀποσκίασμα
verses.king_james: Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, and cometh down from the Father of lights, with whom is no variableness, neither shadow of turning.

verses.latin_vulgate: Voluntarie enim genuit nos verbo veritatis, ut simus initium aliquod creaturæ ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βουληθεὶς ἀπεκύησεν ἡμᾶς λόγῳ ἀληθείας εἰς τὸ εἶναι ἡμᾶς ἀπαρχήν τινα τῶν αὐτοῦ κτισμάτων
verses.king_james: Of his own will begat he us with the word of truth, that we should be a kind of firstfruits of his creatures.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scitis, fratres mei dilectissimi. Sit autem omnis homo velox ad audiendum: tardus autem ad loquendum, et tardus ad iram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε ἀδελφοί μου ἀγαπητοί ἔστω πᾶς ἄνθρωπος ταχὺς εἰς τὸ ἀκοῦσαι βραδὺς εἰς τὸ λαλῆσαι βραδὺς εἰς ὀργήν
verses.king_james: Wherefore, my beloved brethren, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath:

verses.latin_vulgate: Ira enim viri justitiam Dei non operatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀργὴ γὰρ ἀνδρὸς δικαιοσύνην θεοῦ οὐ κατεργάζεται
verses.king_james: For the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod abjicientes omnem immunditiam, et abundantiam malitiæ, in mansuetudine suscipite insitum verbum, quod potest salvare animas vestras.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ ἀποθέμενοι πᾶσαν ῥυπαρίαν καὶ περισσείαν κακίας ἐν πραΰτητι δέξασθε τὸν ἔμφυτον λόγον τὸν δυνάμενον σῶσαι τὰς ψυχὰς ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Wherefore lay apart all filthiness and superfluity of naughtiness, and receive with meekness the engrafted word, which is able to save your souls.

verses.latin_vulgate: Estote autem factores verbi, et non auditores tantum: fallentes vosmetipsos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γίνεσθε δὲ ποιηταὶ λόγου καὶ μὴ μόνον ἀκροαταὶ παραλογιζόμενοι ἑαυτούς
verses.king_james: But be ye doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving your own selves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia si quis auditor est verbi, et non factor, hic comparabitur viro consideranti vultum nativitatis suæ in speculo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι εἴ τις ἀκροατὴς λόγου ἐστὶν καὶ οὐ ποιητής οὗτος ἔοικεν ἀνδρὶ κατανοοῦντι τὸ πρόσωπον τῆς γενέσεως αὐτοῦ ἐν ἐσόπτρῳ
verses.king_james: For if any be a hearer of the word, and not a doer, he is like unto a man beholding his natural face in a glass:

verses.latin_vulgate: consideravit enim se, et abiit, et statim oblitus est qualis fuerit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατενόησεν γὰρ ἑαυτὸν καὶ ἀπελήλυθεν καὶ εὐθέως ἐπελάθετο ὁποῖος ἦν
verses.king_james: For he beholdeth himself, and goeth his way, and straightway forgetteth what manner of man he was.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem perspexerit in legem perfectam libertatis, et permanserit in ea, non auditor obliviosus factus, sed factor operis: hic beatus in facto suo erit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ παρακύψας εἰς νόμον τέλειον τὸν τῆς ἐλευθερίας καὶ παραμείνας οὗτος οὐκ ἀκροατὴς ἐπιλησμονῆς γενόμενος ἀλλὰ ποιητὴς ἔργου οὗτος μακάριος ἐν τῇ ποιήσει αὐτοῦ ἔσται
verses.king_james: But whoso looketh into the perfect law of liberty, and continueth therein, he being not a forgetful hearer, but a doer of the work, this man shall be blessed in his deed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis autem putat se religiosum esse, non refrenans linguam suam, sed seducens cor suum, hujus vana est religio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴ τις δοκεῖ θρησκὸς εἶναι ἐν ὑμῖν μὴ χαλιναγωγῶν γλῶσσαν αὐτοῦ ἀλλ᾽ ἀπατῶν καρδίαν αὐτοῦ τούτου μάταιος ἡ θρησκεία
verses.king_james: If any man among you seem to be religious, and bridleth not his tongue, but deceiveth his own heart, this man's religion is vain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Religio munda et immaculata apud Deum et Patrem, hæc est: visitare pupillos et viduas in tribulatione eorum, et immaculatum se custodire ab hoc sæculo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θρησκεία καθαρὰ καὶ ἀμίαντος παρὰ τῷ θεῷ καὶ πατρὶ αὕτη ἐστίν ἐπισκέπτεσθαι ὀρφανοὺς καὶ χήρας ἐν τῇ θλίψει αὐτῶν ἄσπιλον ἑαυτὸν τηρεῖν ἀπὸ τοῦ κόσμου
verses.king_james: Pure religion and undefiled before God and the Father is this, To visit the fatherless and widows in their affliction, and to keep himself unspotted from the world.
